what: The one rule: key statistic by the ARD’s context
title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: pivot_across(statistic = list(...)) matches list names ARD’s context column verbatim. value depends function built ARD — use wrong key rows dropped silently. Always check unique(ard$context) first. single string, statistic = list(default = ...), applies one format every context.

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: cdisc_saf_demo_ard stacked continuous categorical block, carries continuous categorical contexts: decimals = c(p = 0) gives integer percent pharma <1 / >99 thresholds; n = 0 cells collapse bare "0" automatically. widened frame now ready tabular() — see Structure.
code: # how it was built (data-raw): cdisc_saf_demo_ard <- cards::ard_stack(   adsl,   .by = TRT01A,   cards::ard_continuous(     variables = c(AGE, BMI),     statistic = ~ cards::continuous_summary_fns(c(       "N",       "mean",       "sd",       "median",       "min",       "max"     ))   ),   cards::ard_categorical(variables = c(AGEGR1, SEX, RACE)) ) data(cdisc_saf_demo_ard, package = "tabular") unique(cdisc_saf_demo_ard$context) #> [1] "continuous"  "categorical" "tabulate"  wide <- pivot_across(   cdisc_saf_demo_ard,   statistic = list(     continuous = c(       N = "{N}",       "Mean (SD)" = "{mean} ({sd})",       Median = "{median}",       "Min, Max" = "{min}, {max}"     ),     categorical = "{n} ({p}%)"   ),   # CDISC precision: SD carries one more decimal than the mean. AGE/BMI are   # whole numbers here (raw precision d = 0), so mean = d + 1 = 1, sd = d + 2 = 2,   # median = d + 1 = 1, and min/max keep the raw precision (0).   decimals = c(mean = 1, sd = 2, median = 1, min = 0, max = 0, p = 0) ) head(wide, 8) #>   variable  stat_label      Placebo Xanomeline High Dose Xanomeline Low Dose #> 1      AGE           N           86                   72                  96 #> 2      AGE   Mean (SD)  75.2 (8.59)          73.8 (7.94)         76.0 (8.11) #> 3      AGE      Median         76.0                 75.5                78.0 #> 4      AGE    Min, Max       52, 89               56, 88              51, 88 #> 5   WEIGHT           N           86                   72                  95 #> 6   WEIGHT   Mean (SD) 62.8 (12.77)         69.5 (14.35)        68.0 (14.50) #> 7   WEIGHT      Median         60.6                 69.0                66.7 #> 8   WEIGHT    Min, Max       34, 86              44, 108             42, 106 #>          Total #> 1          254 #> 2  75.1 (8.25) #> 3         77.0 #> 4       51, 89 #> 5          253 #> 6 66.6 (14.13) #> 7         66.7 #> 8      34, 108

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: cardx CI functions emit contexts (proportion_ci, continuous_ci) estimate / conf.low / conf.high stats. pivot_across() consumes frame follows cards long-format, can feed cardx ARD build rows hand — useful statistic computed outside cards:
code: # from cardx: orr_ard <- cardx::ard_categorical_ci(   resp,   by = "TRT01A",   variables = "RESP",   method = "clopper-pearson",   conf.level = 0.95 ) # equivalent hand-built ARD (one row group, proportion_ci context) arms <- c("Placebo", "Xanomeline Low Dose", "Xanomeline High Dose") orr_ard <- expand.grid(   group1_level = arms,   stat_name = c("estimate", "conf.low", "conf.high"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) orr_ard$group1 <- "TRT01A" orr_ard$variable <- "RESP" orr_ard$variable_level <- "Responders" orr_ard$context <- "proportion_ci" orr_ard$stat <- c(0.62, 0.55, 0.48, 0.50, 0.42, 0.36, 0.73, 0.67, 0.60)  pivot_across(   orr_ard,   statistic = list(proportion_ci = "{estimate} ({conf.low}, {conf.high})"),   decimals = c(estimate = 3, conf.low = 3, conf.high = 3) ) #>   variable   stat_label              Placebo  Xanomeline Low Dose #> 1     RESP   Responders 0.620 (0.500, 0.730) 0.550 (0.420, 0.670) #>   Xanomeline High Dose #> 1 0.480 (0.360, 0.600)

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: hierarchical ARD widens soc / label / row_type triple (single variable), ready indented SOC ▸ PT layout: (Turning soc/label/indent indented stub Structure.)
code: data(cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard, package = "tabular") ae <- pivot_across(cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard, statistic = "{n} ({p}%)") ae$indent <- as.integer(ae$row_type == "pt") # depth for col_spec(indent_by=) head(ae, 6) #>                                      soc                                  label #> 1           ..ard_hierarchical_overall..           ..ard_hierarchical_overall.. #> 2 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS #> 3 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                               PRURITUS #> 4 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                               ERYTHEMA #> 5 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                                   RASH #> 6 SKIN AND SUBCUTANEOUS TISSUE DISORDERS                          HYPERHIDROSIS #>   row_type  Placebo Xanomeline High Dose Xanomeline Low Dose indent #> 1  overall 52 (60%)             66 (92%)            81 (84%)      0 #> 2      soc 19 (22%)             35 (49%)            36 (38%)      0 #> 3       pt   8 (9%)             25 (35%)            21 (22%)      1 #> 4       pt   8 (9%)             14 (19%)            14 (15%)      1 #> 5       pt   5 (6%)              8 (11%)            13 (14%)      1 #> 6       pt   2 (2%)              8 (11%)              4 (4%)      1

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: ard_stack(.= c(ARM, SEX)) carries second grouping variable. Name row_group = pivot_across() widens leading row column (rather mis-reading SOC/PT hierarchy). cards long shape stack produces (RACE ARM within SEX), built hand example runs without cards: 2-variable .present must say variable arm column (column =) row dimension (row_group =); SEX column composes subgroup() col_spec(usage = "group") downstream. cards encodes crossing factor real hierarchy identically, declaration disambiguates — leave row_group unset genuine SOC/PT hierarchy.
code: two_by_ard <- expand.grid(   group1_level = c("Placebo", "Drug"),   group2_level = c("F", "M"),   variable_level = c("WHITE", "BLACK"),   stat_name = c("n", "p"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) two_by_ard$group1 <- "ARM" two_by_ard$group2 <- "SEX" two_by_ard$variable <- "RACE" two_by_ard$context <- "categorical" two_by_ard$stat <- ifelse(two_by_ard$stat_name == "n", 10, 0.25)  pivot_across(   two_by_ard,   column = "ARM",   row_group = "SEX",   statistic = list(categorical = "{n} ({p}%)") ) #>   SEX variable stat_label  Placebo     Drug #> 1   F     RACE      WHITE 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 2   F     RACE      BLACK 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 3   M     RACE      WHITE 10 (25%) 10 (25%) #> 4   M     RACE      BLACK 10 (25%) 10 (25%)

title: Data in: the cards → tabular pipeline
text: one thing get right: key statistic ARD’s context. Inspect unique(ard$context) key list match (pass single string / default =). explicitly-supplied statistic matches context, pivot_across() warns rather silently falling back {n}.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: emit() writes file, dispatching extension: as_grid(spec) resolves fully-laid-grid without writing file — useful testing programmatic inspection.
code: emit(spec, "table.rtf") # RTF emit(spec, "table.html") # HTML emit(spec, "table.docx") # Word emit(spec, "table.pdf") # PDF (via LaTeX) emit(spec, "table.md") # Markdown

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: One spec renders every backend, page-oriented features differ: ¹ HTML/MD one continuous document; browser repeats <thead> print. ² HTML/MD per-page N renders row subgroup banner instead repeating header.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: RTF, HTML, DOCX, Markdown need nothing beyond R package. PDF system dependency — LaTeX engine plus packages backend uses (tabularray, ninecolors, siunitx, tex-gyre, …). Check install: OS-managed TeX Live (RHEL/dnf, Debian/apt): tlmgr locked refuses install (“likely destroy … TeXLive install”). force --ignore-warning. Install user-space TinyTeX control instead: tinytex::install_tinytex(), restart R, tlmgr_install(...). decimal alignment paper backends, metric-compatible fonts matter — check check_fonts().
code: check_latex() # reports what's missing + the exact command tinytex::tlmgr_install(c("tabularray", "ninecolors", "siunitx", "tex-gyre"))

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: relative DOCX path works. emit(spec, "/x.docx") resolves path working directory like every backend (output path absolutised OOXML zip staged). normalizePath() dance needed. PDF build appears hang, LaTeX engine stopping interactive error prompt — fix underlying LaTeX dependency (run check_latex()); render RTF/HTML keep working meantime.

title: Output & qualification: backends, requirements, and the CDISC pilot
text: package ships qualification (inst/qualification/) rebuilds representative CDISC-pilot tables (demographics, populations, AE overview, AE SOC/PT) public PHUSE Test Data Factory ADaM renders every backend, checking three things per cell: emits without error, file structurally valid, independent count computed ADaM appears rendered text backend (cross-backend content parity). four pilot tables across four file backends give 16/16 PASS matrix: ¹ PDF column verified manually local environment LaTeX engine (check_latex()) pdftotext text-parity check. run continuous integration, carries TeX install; CI exercises RTF, HTML, DOCX backends. runnable script (inst/qualification/qualify_tabular_cdisc.R) fetch data qualification README inst/qualification/ folder; direct evidence one tabular spec produces consistent, correct output across backends.

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: Multi-line titles static footnotes arguments tabular():   Table 14-2.01 Demographic Baseline Characteristics ITT Population   Percentages based number ITT subjects per arm. BMI = body mass index. anchored footnote (auto-numbered superscript specific cell header) use footnote() cells_*() location: Excludes one subject withdrawn dosing.
code: tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14-2.01",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "ITT Population"   ),   footnotes = c(     "Percentages are based on the number of ITT subjects per arm.",     "BMI = body mass index."   ) ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal")) base |>   footnote(     "Excludes one subject withdrawn before dosing.",     .at = cells_headers(j = "Total")   )

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: Regulatory TFLs carry header every page (protocol, page X Y, data-cut). preset page chrome, title. pagehead / pagefoot take left / right (center) vectors; tokens {page}, {npages}, {program}, {datetime} resolve render time: running header/footer page chrome — repeats every page paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX) appear single-page HTML preview . Emit paged backend see : Stacking direction. vector stacks outward table: index 1 line nearest table body, later elements move toward page edge. header reads “Analysis Set” top “Protocol” just table, put "Protocol…" first. (Easy invert — check rendered page.)
code: chrome <- base |>   preset(     pagehead = list(       left = c("Analysis Set: Safety", "Protocol: XYZ-123"),       right = c("Data cut: 2026-01-15", "Page {page} of {npages}")     )   ) chrome emit(chrome, "demographics.pdf") # protocol + page x of y on every page

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: style() appends one location + attribute layer. Target region cells_*() constructor; attributes include bold, italic, underline, color, background, borders via brdr(): Body filters live cells_body(): = (row index), j = (column name), = (expression data).
code: base |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_headers()) |> # bold the header band   style(italic = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |> # italic section rows   style(background = "#F2F2F2", .at = cells_body(j = "Total")) # shade the Total column

title: Presentation: titles, footnotes, page chrome, and styling
text: preset() carries cosmetic defaults — fonts, rules, padding, na_text, paper/orientation/margins — width_mode decides table fills page: Use set_preset() top study make defaults apply every table without restating . Decimal alignment (col_spec(align = "decimal")) pads numeric cells non-breaking spaces decimal points line . Alignment exact monospaced font; proportional fonts visually close (em-aware padding future work). submission TFLs Courier exact.
code: base |>   preset(     font_size = 9,     orientation = "landscape",     paper_size = "letter",     margins = c(1, 0.75, 1, 0.75),     width_mode = "window",     na_text = "-"   )

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: reference safety table: usage = "group" parameter stub, indented statistic rows, decimal-aligned arm columns, population BigN folded header cdisc_saf_n.   Table 14.1.1 Demographic Baseline Characteristics Safety Population   Percentages based number subjects per treatment group.
code:
n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  demo_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Percentages are based on the number of subjects per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   )  demo_tbl emit(demo_tbl, "t_14_1_1.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: flat overview stat_label carries high-level flag rows two-space-indented maximum-severity detail rows; one usage = "group" stat_label drives levels.   Table 14.3.0 Overview Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events Safety Population
code:
ae_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.0",     "Overview of Treatment-Emergent Adverse Events",     "Safety Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "group", label = ""),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   )  ae_tbl emit(ae_tbl, "t_14_3_0.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

what: Adverse events by SOC and preferred term
title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: two-level hierarchy: label holds SOC text SOC rows PT text PT rows, indented indent_level; hidden soc / row_type / n_total / soc_n columns ride along partition sort keys. sort_rows() clusters PTs parent SOC orders levels descending frequency.   Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events System Organ Class Preferred Term Safety Population   table long, real deliverable paginates — keeping SOC preferred terms together. Pagination materialises paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX), add paginate() emit one :
code:
aesocpt_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_aesocpt,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     label = col_spec(       label = "SOC / Preferred Term",       indent_by = "indent_level"     ),     soc = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}", align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE))  aesocpt_tbl aesocpt_tbl |>   paginate(keep_together = "soc", continuation = "(continued)") |>   emit("t_14_3_1.pdf")

what: Best overall response and response rates
title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: efficacy companion: group_label synthesises one bold section band per analysis block (best overall response, ORR, CBR, DCR) via header_row, indented response rows beneath. Denominators come cdisc_eff_n.   Table 14.2.1 Best Overall Response Response Rates Efficacy Evaluable Population
code:
ne <- stats::setNames(cdisc_eff_n$n, cdisc_eff_n$arm_short)  resp_tbl <- tabular(   cdisc_eff_resp,   titles = c(     "Table 14.2.1",     "Best Overall Response and Response Rates",     "Efficacy Evaluable Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     group_label = col_spec(usage = "group", group_display = "header_row"),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "Response"),     groupid = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={ne['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={ne['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={ne['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     )   )  resp_tbl emit(resp_tbl, "t_14_2_1.rtf") # ship: or .pdf / .docx / .html / .md

title: Recipes: the CDISC-pilot tables, end to end
text: Data — produce wide frames cards/cardx ARD pivot_across(). Structure Presentation — column, pagination, styling verbs recipe draws . Output & qualification — cross-backend validation recipes mirror.

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: Every column gets role via col_spec(usage = …). Picking right one single important structural decision:   Demographics   cols_apply() attaches one shared col_spec arm columns — use instead repeating cols(placebo = …, drug_50 = …) variable number arms. pivot_across() already indents nested levels. Categorical level labels come pivot_across() leading indent baked string. also set usage = "indent" get double indent — either keep labels -(usage = "display"/"id") trimws() first let engine indent. Don’t .
code: data(cdisc_saf_demo, package = "tabular") arms <- c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal"))

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: (N=…) denominator goes arm’s header label. Build BigN table interpolate glue:   Demographics   Clinical convention: BigN population denominator (ADSL), number rows domain dataset — compute population, summarised data. variable number arms, per-arm label one cols_apply() call instead hand-written line : {.name} token resolves matched column’s name, rest {…} evaluates calling environment, BigN looks :   Demographics
code:
data(cdisc_saf_n, package = "tabular") N <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = ""),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
(N={N['placebo']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
(N={N['drug_50']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
(N={N['drug_100']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total
(N={N['Total']})", align = "decimal")   ) arm_cols <- c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total")  tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "")   ) |>   cols_apply(     arm_cols,     col_spec(label = "{.name}
(N={N[.name]})", align = "decimal")   )

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: headers() builds spanning bands groups columns:   Demographics   Widths: "auto" (default) sizes content; pinned value ("1in", 1.0, "20%") wraps within width. Set shared arm width via cols_apply() last — non-default width wins field-merge; later cols() call carrying default width = "auto" otherwise ambiguous.
code: tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "", width = "2.2in")   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal", width = "1in")) |>   headers("Treatment Group" = c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"))

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: paginate() derives rows-per-page budget preset (paper, font, margins) title/footnote/header line counts — never set rows-per-page hand. keep_together stops page break landing inside section’s run:   AEs SOC PT   preview one continuous table: row pagination, keep_together, continuation marker materialise paged backends (RTF, PDF, DOCX), HTML. Emit one see page breaks:
code: data(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, package = "tabular") ae_pages <- tabular(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, titles = "AEs by SOC and PT") |>   cols(     label = col_spec(       label = "SOC / Preferred Term",       indent_by = "indent_level"     ),     soc = col_spec(       usage = "group",       visible = FALSE,       group_display = "column_repeat"     ),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE)   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   paginate(     keep_together = "soc",     orphan_floor = 4,     widow_floor = 2,     continuation = "(continued)"   ) ae_pages emit(ae_pages, "ae_soc_pt.pdf") # continuation marker repeats on each continued page

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: columns don’t fit one page, paginate(panels = N) splits non-group columns N chunks repeats every group/id column panel (row labels reappear). Make row label usage = "id" rides every panel:   Demographics (wide split)   Panels paged-backend feature: HTML Markdown table stays one continuous block (preview ), RTF, PDF, DOCX place panel page id / group columns repeated. Emit paged backend see split: Two things know: panels = N splits N equal chunks — explicit split position (“first 5, rest”). Equal split fine page-fit; need specific boundary, known limitation. panels = "auto" currently -op (treated 1): auto-fit, just leaves every column one page (table may overflow warning). Always pass explicit integer.
code: wide_split <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics (wide split)") |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "") # repeats on every panel   ) |>   cols_apply(arms, col_spec(align = "decimal")) |>   paginate(panels = 2, continuation = "(continued)") wide_split emit(wide_split, "demographics_wide.pdf") # panel 2 carries the (continued) marker

title: Structure: columns, headers, and pagination
text: subgroup() partitions table — one page block per value, banner hard page break. partition-constant column can ride banner:   Vital signs sex   different (N=) per arm page (column headers re-resolving per subgroup), pass big_n — small table N per page × arm. bundled dataset carries per-arm-per-page counts, build inline (also shape big_n expects):   Vital signs sex   big_n accepts wide shape (page column + one column per arm) long count()-style table (page, arm, n).
code: data(cdisc_saf_subgroup, package = "tabular") tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital signs by sex") |>   cols(     sex = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     sex_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Statistic")   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   subgroup(by = "sex", label = "Sex: {sex} (N = {sex_n})") # page total in banner big_n <- data.frame(   sex = c("F", "M"),   placebo = c(53L, 33L),   drug_50 = c(50L, 46L),   drug_100 = c(40L, 32L),   Total = c(143L, 111L) )  tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup, titles = "Vital signs by sex") |>   cols(     sex_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr_n = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "id", label = "Statistic")   ) |>   cols_apply(     c("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"),     col_spec(align = "decimal")   ) |>   subgroup(by = "sex", label = "Sex: {sex}", big_n = big_n) # per-page (N=) per arm

title: Get started with tabular
text: Start wide frame — bundled demographics summary, one row per statistic, one column per treatment arm: Describe columns. spec prints live HTML table — render emit() produces, shown inline:   Table 14-2.01 Demographic Baseline Characteristics ITT Population   write file, hand spec emit(); backend chosen file extension (explicit format =): whole loop: wide frame → tabular() → cols() → emit() — one spec, backend.
code: data(cdisc_saf_demo, package = "tabular") head(cdisc_saf_demo) #>           variable stat_label     placebo    drug_100     drug_50       Total #> 1      Age (years)          n          86          72          96         254 #> 2      Age (years)  Mean (SD) 75.2 (8.59) 73.8 (7.94) 76.0 (8.11) 75.1 (8.25) #> 3      Age (years)     Median        76.0        75.5        78.0        77.0 #> 4      Age (years)     Q1, Q3  69.2, 81.8  70.5, 79.0  71.0, 82.0  70.0, 81.0 #> 5      Age (years)   Min, Max      52, 89      56, 88      51, 88      51, 89 #> 6 Age Group, n (%)      18-64   14 (16.3)   11 (15.3)     8 (8.3)   33 (13.0) spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14-2.01",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "ITT Population"   ) ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(       usage = "group",       group_display = "header_row",       label = ""     ),     stat_label = col_spec(usage = "indent", label = "")   )  spec out <- tempfile(fileext = ".rtf") emit(spec, out) # RTF here; swap to .docx / .pdf / .html / .md file.exists(out) #> [1] TRUE

title: Get started with tabular
text: Read left right. summarise upstream — cards/cardx, dplyr, SAS — long ARD, widen display-ready frame pivot_across(), hand tabular. Inside package work happens three phases (Build → Resolve → Emit), resolved spec emits every backend, HTML preview RTF ship can never disagree.  tabular’s pipeline: summarise upstream long ARD, widen pivot_across(), build, resolve, emit one immutable spec every backend.

what: Anatomy of a clinical table page
title: Get started with tabular
text: submission table just grid numbers — page four stacked sections, reviewer expects one place. Every tabular verb maps onto piece picture:  four-section clinical page verb fills section. Header section — running protocol, optional status, page x y, set page chrome preset(pagehead =, pagefoot =). Title lines — table number four centred titles, passed tabular(titles =). Data section — optional subgroup() banner, column-header band built cols() headers(), decimal-aligned data. Footnote lines — static footnotes = plus auto-numbered footnote() markers, program path, name, timestamp. preset() frames four controlling page geometry (paper, orientation, margins, fonts).

title: Get started with tabular
text: rest docs task-oriented — read one matches : Data — turn cards/cardx ARD (long ARD) wide frame, pivot_across(). Structure — columns, headers, BigN, splitting wide long tables across pages (cols(), headers(), paginate(), subgroup()). Presentation — titles, footnotes, running headers, cell styling (footnote(), preset(), style()). Recipes — canonical CDISC-pilot safety efficacy tables built end end, rendered live. Output & qualification — backends, system requirements, CDISC-pilot cross-backend validation.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: tabular turns pre-summarised data frame submission-grade clinical table emits natively RTF, PDF, HTML, LaTeX, DOCX — Java, LibreOffice, Word automation. One short pipeline gives decimal alignment via real font metrics, multi-level column headers, predicate-targeted styling, group-aware pagination, built CDISC ADaM workflows FDA / EMA / PMDA submissions. R table package pairs live HTML preview paginated print deliverable: spec eyeball notebook one paginates RTF ship. Scope. tabular renders tables listings today. Figure (graph) output yet supported focus next release.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: Install released version CRAN: development version GitHub: R dependencies install automatically. five backends differ else need: PDF backend shells . Install tinytex per machine tabular compiles xelatex thereafter: check_latex() reports LaTeX packages present prints exact tlmgr_install() line anything missing; check_fonts(spec) fonts spec asks , per backend. TeX Live managed OS. TeX Live came system package manager (RHEL dnf, Debian/Ubuntu apt), tlmgr usually locked tlmgr_install() fails permissions. Install user-space TinyTeX alongside rather fighting system copy — never reach --ignore-warning force .
code: install.packages("tabular") # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("vthanik/tabular") # or remotes::install_github("vthanik/tabular") install.packages("tinytex") tinytex::install_tinytex() # one-time TeX setup tinytex::tlmgr_install(c("tabularray", "ninecolors", "siunitx", "tex-gyre")) tabular::check_latex()   # PDF readiness, with the install remedy

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: pipeline starts pre-summarised wide data frame (one row = one display row — tabular aggregation) chains one verb per concern. Every verb returns updated, immutable tabular_spec; engine resolves render time. tab emits every backend one spec. table tabular’s HTML render — identical spec also produces RTF, paginated PDF, tabularray LaTeX fragment, native OOXML .docx:
code: library(tabular)  # BigN denominators, keyed by arm n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  # columns render in data-frame order, so put them in dose order first; # subset to Age / Sex / Race for a compact display keep <- c("Age (years)", "Sex, n (%)", "Race, n (%)") demo <- cdisc_saf_demo[   cdisc_saf_demo$variable %in% keep,   c("variable", "stat_label", "placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total") ]  tab <- tabular(   demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographic and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Percentages are based on the number of subjects per treatment group." ) |>   cols(     variable = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo = col_spec(       label = "Placebo (N={n['placebo']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50 (N={n['drug_50']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100 = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100 (N={n['drug_100']})",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total = col_spec(label = "Total (N={n['Total']})", align = "decimal")   )  # render to any backend by file extension (or format = "...") path <- emit(tab, tempfile(fileext = ".rtf")) # submission deliverable

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: Five native backends, one spec. emit() dispatches file extension RTF 1.9.1, PDF (via tinytex), self-contained Bootstrap HTML, tabularray LaTeX, native OOXML DOCX. JVM, Office round-trip. Decimal alignment survives page. Numbers align decimal using backend’s real font metrics, guessed padding — columns stay aligned print, just screen. Submission chrome built . Multi-line titles, eleven footnote lines, page header/footer slots, four-section page layout regulatory reviewers expect. Auto-numbered footnotes. footnote() anchors marker cell, header, title; engine assigns glyph , reading order, deduped id, byte-identical across every backend page. Group-aware pagination. Keep SOC preferred terms one page, repeat titles/headers/footnotes per page, control orphan/widow rows, split wide tables horizontal panels. Display-design. tabular styles renders; never filters, aggregates, weights. Pair cards / gtsummary / dplyr / SAS upstream feed tidy wide frame. QC trail. emit(data_file = ...) writes resolved wide data beside render, CDISC ARS audit manifest documents display.

title: Clinical TFL rendering for R
text: tabular renderer pre-summarised clinical tables, statistics engine. Compute summary upstream — cards, gtsummary, dplyr, SAS — hand finished wide frame tabular(). Reach gtsummary rtables want package compute summary; reach tabular render summary already submission-grade output. matrix reflects package’s documented export surface (verified namespaces; via gt means gtsummary renders gt): Two notes marks: Live HTML preview means table renders HTML inline print Quarto / R Markdown chunk RStudio viewer (knit_print method). rtables prints monospace ASCII table default ships knit_print method, — ; can still emit HTML explicit as_html() call. PDF compiled LaTeX, needs TeX installation — see Installation . Every backend pure R.

title: Convert a tabular_spec to an htmltools tagList — as.tags.tabular_spec

code: # ---- Example 1: Embed two tables in a custom htmltools page ---- # # Compose two tabular tables in one parent container. `as.tags(spec)` # is the entry point `print()` and `knit_print()` use under the hood. # Wrap the `tagList` in `htmltools::browsable()` so it renders as live # HTML in a viewer / Quarto chunk / pkgdown page instead of printing # its source, the same convention `gt` and `flextable` follow. s1 <- tabular(cdisc_saf_demo, titles = "Demographics") s2 <- tabular(cdisc_saf_ae, titles = "AE overall")  if (requireNamespace("htmltools", quietly = TRUE)) { what: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Runs full engine pipeline spec returns resolved tabular_grid — intermediate object emit() hands backend. Pure function: files written, global state touched. Use development inspect emit() pass downstream, building custom backend, piping resolved grid non-file consumer (e.g. inline preview chunk Quarto notebook).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code: as_grid(.spec)

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: .spec tabular_spec resolve. <tabular_spec>: required. Built verb chain (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset()).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: tabular_grid S7 object. Two slots: @pages — list one entry per display page. entry named list pagination fields (page_index, panel_index, is_continuation, continuation, show_titles, repeat_headers, show_footnotes_here), row + column slice indices (row_indices, col_indices, col_names), sliced cell text (cells_text — character matrix), sliced inline ASTs (cells_ast — list-matrix inline_ast), sliced style nodes (cells_style — list-matrix style_node), column-label ASTs visible columns (col_labels_ast). @metadata — per-table information backends consume per render: format (resolved backend tag, NA_character_ as_grid() calls), rows_per_page, total_pages, total_panels, nrow_data, ncol_data, col_names, cols (original col_spec() entries keyed column name), headers (flattened header band grid), titles, footnotes, titles_ast, footnotes_ast, col_labels_ast, pagehead_ast / pagefoot_ast (resolved page-band content — NULL active preset declares band, otherwise list(left, center, right) length-N lists inline_ast N = row count index 1 body-edge row).

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
text: Engine pipeline order load-bearing. Phases run fixed order; order matters phase reads post- previous-phase state spec: engine_sort() — apply sort spec. engine_headers() — validate header tree flatten band grid. engine_style() — evaluate every style predicate post-sort data grid. predicate may reference column spec@data. engine_format() — apply per-column formats, substitute na_text, parse every cell / title / footnote / label parse_inline() inline_ast. engine_decimal() — column-wide decimal alignment column flagged col_spec(align = "decimal"). Operates formatted text; output character matrix NBSP padding inserted decimal marks line . engine_paginate() — split pages (vertical row chunks + horizontal panel chunks). plan drives per-page slicing cells / styles / ASTs . grid backend contract. Every backend (backend_md, future backend_html, etc.) consumes tabular_grid — never tabular_spec. New backends need walk grid@pages grid@metadata; engine pipeline fixed dependency never re-implement. /O. as_grid() writes nothing disk touches global state. safe call repeatedly interactive exploration; cost roughly one emit() without backend write step.

title: Resolve a tabular_spec into a tabular_grid — as_grid
code:
# ---- Example 1: Demographics — inspect the resolved grid ---- # # Resolve the canonical safety-pop demographics pipeline into a # `tabular_grid` and inspect what `emit()` would hand a backend. # The first page's `cells_text` matrix is the decimal-aligned # output as the backend would render it; the metadata carries the # pagination plan + header / title / footnote ASTs. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  demo <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = c(     "Table 14.1.1",     "Demographics and Baseline Characteristics",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Source: ADSL." ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("variable", "stat_label"))  demo_grid <- as_grid(demo) demo_grid@metadata$total_pages #> [1] 2 demo_grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text[1:3, c("stat_label", "placebo")] #>      stat_label    placebo         #> [1,] "Age (years)" ""              #> [2,] "  Mean (SD)" " 75.2 (8.59) " #> [3,] "  Median"    " 76.0        "  # ---- Example 2: AE-by-SOC/PT paginated grid — verify the split ---- # # Same shape as Example 1 plus pagination protecting the SOC # grouping. With a tight font size the grid carries multiple page # entries; concatenating each page's `row_indices` reconstructs # the full data, and every page carries the full header band grid # at `grid@metadata$headers` so backends can re-render the header # on every continuation page. ae <- cdisc_saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total))  ae_spec <- tabular(   ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(usage = "group", visible = FALSE,                         group_display = "column_repeat"),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |>   paginate(keep_together = "soc")  ae_grid <- as_grid(ae_spec) length(ae_grid@pages) #> [1] 3  # ---- Example 3: Subgroup partition — one page set per group ---- # # When `subgroup()` is attached, `as_grid()` runs the resolve # pipeline once per group and concatenates the pages. `cdisc_saf_subgroup` # carries `sex` as a natural partition axis; inspect # `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_index` and `@pages[[i]]$subgroup_line_ast` # to confirm each page knows its group identity and banner text. # `sex` auto-hides as the partition `by` column; no explicit # `col_spec(visible = FALSE)` needed. sg_spec <- tabular(cdisc_saf_subgroup) |>   cols(     agegr      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Age Group"),     sex_n      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     agegr_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     paramcd    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     param      = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Parameter"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic")   ) |>   subgroup("sex")  sg_grid <- as_grid(sg_spec) length(sg_grid@pages) #> [1] 2 vapply(sg_grid@pages, function(p) p$subgroup_index %||% NA_integer_, integer(1)) #> [1] 1 2  # ---- Example 4: Pre-flight inspection before emit() ---- # # Resolve a spec to its grid without writing anywhere. Useful in # tests, for snapshotting cell text under different presets, or # for spec-introspection inside higher-level wrappers that need # to know how many pages a render will produce. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) demog_spec <- tabular(   cdisc_saf_demo,   titles = "Demographics" ) |>   cols(     variable   = col_spec(usage = "group", label = "Characteristic"),     stat_label = col_spec(label = "Statistic"),     placebo    = col_spec(       label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_50    = col_spec(       label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     drug_100   = col_spec(       label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}",       align = "decimal"     ),     Total      = col_spec(       label = "Total
N={n['Total']}",       align = "decimal"     )   ) grid <- as_grid(demog_spec) length(grid@pages) #> [1] 2 dim(grid@pages[[1]]$cells_text) #> [1] 49  5

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Build small immutable record describing one border line — width, style, colour. brdr() value stroke hand preset() rules knob (one entry per rule name, e.g. rules = list(midrule = brdr(width = 0.75))) style()'s border arguments (style(border_top = brdr(...), .= cells_table(side = "rows"))). Successive preset() calls layer cleanly, one-override composes onto house-style template without disturbing rules.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
code: brdr(width = "thin", style = "solid", color = "ink")  is_brdr(x)

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: width Stroke width. <numeric(1) | character(1)>: default "thin"*. Either numeric points (>= 0) one four named keywords ("hairline", "thin", "medium", "thick"`). style Line style. <character(1)>: default "solid"*. One "solid", "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none"`. color Stroke colour. <character(1)>: default "currentColor"*. Hex ("#RRGGBB"), CSS colour name, "currentColor"` inherit surrounding text colour. x R object — tested is_brdr() membership tabular_brdr S3 class.

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: tabular_brdr S3 object — length-3 named list suitable preset(rules = list(<rule> = .)) style(border_* = .).

title: Border-line specification — brdr
text: Surface. single tabular_brdr value length-3 named list class "tabular_brdr": list(style, width, color). shape identical bare triple style()'s per-side scalars accept, resolver R/borders.R can ingest either form transparently. Construct brdr(); test is_brdr(). Width keywords. width accepts either numeric points (typical clinical values: 0.25, 0.5, 1, 1.5) one four named keywords: Keywords resolve numeric points immediately; constructed value carries numeric width. Numeric inputs pass unchanged non-negative check. Style enum. style one "solid" (default), "dashed", "dotted", "double", "dashdot", "none". "none" explicit clear--rule sentinel: setting rule brdr(style = "none") (bare string "none") preset()(rules = list(...)) suppresses baseline rule backend otherwise draw. Color. Hex ("#212529"), CSS colour name ("black", "slategray"), "ink" token (default; resolves primary rule ink #212529, decoupled surrounding text colour recoloured header keeps neutral rule), "currentColor" (inherit surrounding text colour per backend convention — w:color="auto" DOCX, document text colour RTF, CSS currentColor keyword HTML).

title: Border-line specification — brdr

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — cdisc_eff_estimates
text: Four competing efficacy models treatment-effect point estimate, 95% confidence-interval bounds, nominal p-value. Shaped numeric-cell table (one row per model) rather usual pre-formatted character cells, exercises col_spec(format = ...) + col_spec(na_text = ...) cascade. One row (MMRM) carries NA CI bounds demonstrate na_text.

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — cdisc_eff_estimates
code: cdisc_eff_estimates

title: Treatment-effect estimates by model — cdisc_eff_estimates
text: data frame 4 rows 5 columns: model Model name ("ANCOVA", "MMRM", "Cox PH", "Bootstrap (1000 reps)"). estimate Numeric point estimate. lower_ci, upper_ci Numeric 95% CI bounds. MMRM row NA bounds. p_value Nominal p-value (numeric).

what: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
text: Pre-summarised efficacy table. Per-arm counts best overall response (BOR) per CDISC category, plus derived ORR, CBR, DCR rate rows followed exact (Clopper-Pearson) 95% CI row. Four sections (Best Overall Response, Objective Response Rate, Clinical Benefit Rate, Disease Control Rate) encoded via groupid + group_label pair single usage = "group" / group_display = "header_row" group_label synthesises one bold section band per groupid block; body rows render band via usage = "indent" stat_label.

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
code: cdisc_eff_resp

title: Best Overall Response and Response Rates — cdisc_eff_resp
text: data frame 13 rows 7 columns: stat_label Row label ("CR", "PR", "SD", "NON-CR/NON-PD", "PD", "NE", "MISSING", "ORR (CR + PR)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "CBR (CR + PR + SD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)", "DCR (CR + PR + SD + NON-CR/NON-PD)", "95% CI (Clopper-Pearson)"). row_type "category" BOR categorical rows, "derived" ORR / CBR / DCR rate rows, "ci" paired confidence-interval rows. Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). placebo, drug_50, drug_100 Per-arm cell text ("n (pct)" rate rows, "(lower, upper)" CI rows). groupid Integer section id (1 = Best Overall Response, 2 = Objective Response Rate, 3 = Clinical Benefit Rate, 4 = Disease Control Rate). Hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE); used section sort / partition key. group_label Character section label, repeating across every row groupid block ("Best Overall Response" x7, "Objective Response Rate" x2, ...). Drives engine's usage = "group" header_row synthesis paired group_display = "header_row".

text: Pre-summarised AE--SOC/PT table. Interleaved row order: overall "TEAE" row first, per-SOC blocks SOC row followed preferred-term detail rows. Top 10 SOCs top 5 PTs per SOC kept; row_type marks role row indent_level carries canonical depth (0 overall SOC, 1 PT) downstream pipeline drives SOC -> PT indent via col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level") without reconstructing every script. richer SOC × PT slice exercises paginate() engine's horizontal-panel splitter end--end realistic submission shell.

title: Adverse events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term — cdisc_saf_aesocpt
text: data frame 61 rows 10 columns: soc System Organ Class label. Repeats across SOC's PT rows; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE) label carries SOC text SOC rows. label row's display label. Equal soc overall SOC-summary rows; equal preferred-term name PT detail rows. Promoted primary display column — pair indent_by = "indent_level" drive SOC -> PT indent. row_type One "overall", "soc", "pt". Partition marker; hide via col_spec(visible = FALSE). indent_level Integer depth (0 overall SOC rows, 1 PT rows). Consumed col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level") label column; engine auto-hides column resolve time. n_total Integer. row's subject count — overall TEAE count overall row, SOC's count SOC row, PT's count PT row. Inner sort key. soc_n Integer. parent SOC's count, broadcast every row SOC's cluster (SOC row + PT children) descending sort soc_n keeps PTs grouped parent. overall row, equal overall TEAE count. Outer sort key. placebo Placebo arm cell text ("n (pct)"). drug_50, drug_100 Drug arms cell text. Total Pooled-across-arms cell text.

Body rows pre-sorted cards-style two-level rule (arrange(desc(soc_n), soc, desc(n_total))) canonical render order already baked ; render-time sort_rows(= c("soc_n", "n_total"), descending = c(TRUE, TRUE)) reproduces via stable sort.

title: Cards hierarchical ARD for AEs by SOC and PT — cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard
text: Long-format companion cdisc_saf_aesocpt. Produced cards::ard_stack_hierarchical() (AEBODSYS, AEDECOD) adsl-level denominators, sorted descending overall incidence via cards::sort_ard_hierarchical(). Limited top-10 SOC, top-5 PT subset cdisc_saf_aesocpt two datasets describe slice data.

title: Cards ARD for demographics (flat ARD companion) — cdisc_saf_demo_ard
text: demographics summary cdisc_saf_demo, long Analysis Results Data (ARD) format produced cards::ard_stack(). One row per (treatment arm, variable, statistic). Shipped teaching dataset shows upstream shape users typically start cards. Convert wide form tabular() accepts via pivot_across() — tabular consume long ARD format, since pre-summarised wide data package boundary.

title: Cards ARD for demographics (flat ARD companion) — cdisc_saf_demo_ard

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: Per-arm subject counts (BigN) safety population, plus Total row. Use table embed BigN inline column headers glue-style {expr} template cols(col_spec(label = ...)); dedicated BigN field col_spec denominator already lives discoverable, joinable form.

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
code: cdisc_saf_n

title: Safety-population BigN per arm — cdisc_saf_n
text: data frame 4 rows 3 columns: arm Raw pharmaverseadam arm label ("Placebo", "Xanomeline Low Dose", "Xanomeline High Dose", "Total"). Matches group1_level _card ARDs (pivot output's column names match setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm) lookup). arm_short Renamed label ("placebo", "drug_50", "drug_100", "Total"). Matches column names cdisc_saf_demo, cdisc_saf_ae, cdisc_saf_aesocpt, cdisc_saf_vital. n Integer subject count.

what: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Pre-summarised vital-signs stats partitioned sex (F / M) age group (<65 / >=65) End--Treatment visit. Two parameters (Systolic BP, Diastolic BP) emit four statistic rows (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). Partition-constant BigN columns (sex_n, agegr_n) ride alongside banners can inline denominator via subgroup(label = "Sex: {sex} (N = {sex_n})") without reaching separate lookup.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
code: cdisc_saf_subgroup

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: data frame 32 rows 11 columns: sex Factor (F / M). agegr Factor (<65 / >=65). sex_n Integer BigN — number subjects partition row's sex (partition-constant; rides banner via {sex_n} template tokens). agegr_n Integer BigN per age group. paramcd CDISC parameter code (SYSBP / DIABP). param Decoded parameter name ("Systolic BP (mmHg)", "Diastolic BP (mmHg)"). stat_label Statistic label (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). placebo, drug_50, drug_100, Total Per-arm cell text.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Derived data-raw/bundle-demo.R pharmaverseadam::advs filtered SAFFL == "Y", three CDISCPILOT01 arms, SYSBP / DIABP parameters, End--Treatment visit.

title: Vital-signs subgroup summary by Sex and Age Group — cdisc_saf_subgroup
text: Designed subgroup() as_grid() examples: two partition axes plus partition-constant BigN columns cover single-variable cohort-style partitions multi-variable (sex × agegr) crossing.

title: Vital-signs summary — cdisc_saf_vital
text: Pre-summarised vital-signs stats. Four parameters (SYSBP, DIABP, PULSE, TEMP) four visits (Baseline, Week 8, Week 16, End Treatment), producing four statistic rows (n, Mean (SD), Median, Min, Max). 4 x 4 x 4 grid makes dataset natural fit paginate() examples — 64 rows comfortably exceed single page typical clinical row-per-page settings.

title: Vital-signs summary — cdisc_saf_vital
