what: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Attach preset_spec tabular_spec, carrying page-geometry knobs (paper, orientation, margins, body font_size + family, h-rule policy, decimal metric, typography defaults). engine consults per-spec preset first computing per-page row budget, decimal-aligned column widths, chrome backend renders around body grid.

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
code: preset(.spec, ..., .template = NULL, .style = NULL, .reset = FALSE)

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: .spec tabular_spec attach preset . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind knob name ... spec slot. ... Named preset knobs. subset preset knobs preset_spec class carries. Knob values validated class's enum / length / type rules; bad values raise tabular_error_input. Unknown knob names raise tabular_error_input recognised set listed. Recognised knobs: font_size — body point size. <numeric(1)>. font_family — body font family. <character | character(1)>. Default "mono". Three accepted shapes: Generic family — "mono" (default), "serif", "sans" (CSS aliases "monospace" / "sans-serif" also recognised). resolver expands per-backend chain leads Linux-installed Liberation face (Posit Workbench / Domino / Citrix / RStudio Server), Microsoft Office face (Courier New / Times New Roman / Arial) desktop Win / Mac consumers, TeX Gyre LaTeX compile, CSS generic HTML. Liberation Mono / Serif / Sans metric-compatible Courier New / TNR / Arial, layout, line breaks, decimal alignment hold across every render context. mono default matches dominant submission-TFL convention deterministic glyph widths drive n (%) cell alignment. Named alias — "Times", "Times New Roman", "Arial", "Helvetica", "Courier", "Courier New". PostScript-era names alias appropriate generic family (Times -> serif, Arial / Helvetica -> sans, Courier -> mono) emit expanded chain. Honours user's intent ("want Times-like rendering") every OS instead hard-erroring Linux server TNR installed. Named font — "Inter", "JetBrains Mono", "Source Serif Pro", sponsor-specific face, etc. Emitted verbatim fallback fabricated. consuming app (browser, xelatex, Word, LibreOffice) resolves name font matcher. RTF DOCX fall back consuming app's substitution table name missing; xelatex hard-errors compile time; HTML browsers fall browser's default font (necessarily class-matched). Explicit stack — c("Inter", "Helvetica", "sans"). User owns chain. Returned verbatim — alias lookup bypassed, c("Times", "Times") honours exact name chain expansion (escape hatch users genuinely want exact-name semantics). Note: Adobe Source Pro longer default lead. Source Pro pre-installed production Linux servers, leading walks 2-3 missing names resolving. Users installed Source Pro can opt via explicit-stack form (c("Source Serif Pro", "serif")). see Word's font dropdown vs. renders. open tabular-generated .rtf Word macOS Windows, font dropdown displays file's requested face — "Liberation Mono" default (Linux-server-installed face). rendered text screen whatever Word's \\*\\falt substitution resolved — typically Courier New macOS / Windows. correct: Liberation Mono Courier New metric-compatible design, rendered layout (line breaks, decimal alignment, page breaks) identical regardless face Word actually used render. \\*\\falt substitution model applies serif (Liberation Serif -> Times New Roman) sans (Liberation Sans -> Arial). force Office names primary. reviewers confused seeing "Liberation Mono" Word font dropdown (cosmetic concern; affect rendering), pass explicit length>1 stack Office name first. resolver returns vector verbatim — alias lookup, chain expansion — RTF file names Office face primary chosen alternate \\*\\falt:   canonical escape hatch authors know consumer audience Mac / Windows Word users want dropdown show Office face directly. orientation — page orientation. <character(1)>. One "landscape" (default), "portrait". paper_size — paper key. <character(1)>. One "letter" (default), "a4". margins — page margins inches. <numeric(1) | numeric(4)>. Length 1 = four sides; length 4 = top, right, bottom, left. pagehead, pagefoot — per-page header / footer band content. <list>. band named list slots left / center / right; every slot name rejected. slot accepts NULL (omit), character scalar, character vector (multi-row content), inline_ast. Empty list() (default) -> band emitted. Single-row form (scalar slots):   Multi-row form (vector slots, index-aligned):   Growth direction. Vector index 1 = body edge; index N = far body. pagehead rows stack upward away table (row closest table index 1). pagefoot rows stack downward away table (row closest table index 1). Shorter slots pad "" FAR end (high index), scalar slot naturally lands body-edge row. Token vocabulary — substituted slot text: {program}, {program_path}, {datetime} resolve per render (as_grid() / emit()); {page} {npages} resolve per page (filled Word / xelatex / browser's print engine view time). program tokens walk 5-mode detection chain — RStudio API, source() frame, Rscript / R CMD BATCH commandArgs (covers Domino + Linux batch + CI), knitr current_input, fallback "<interactive>". rules — single border vocabulary (replaces old borders knob). String sugar "booktabs" (default, clinical baseline), "grid", "frame", "none"; single brdr() broadcast every active rule; named list keyed nine rule names (toprule, midrule, bottomrule, spanrule, rowrule, footnoterule, leftrule, rightrule, colrule) — unlisted rules keep default, bare string "none" drops one. rules = list(rowrule = brdr()) reproduces old hlines = "". bottomrule vs footnoterule. mutually exclusive: exactly one rule sits data -> footnote boundary. default bottomrule (table's bottom edge); footnoterule (table-width rule opening footnote section) default. distinct footnote-section rule, footnoterule drawn paginated backends — RTF, LaTeX / PDF, DOCX. HTML backend continuous (non-paginated) separate footnote section, folds one rule: whichever bottomrule / footnoterule active becomes table's bottom edge (bottomrule wins set). Setting footnoterule therefore still draws closing rule HTML — rendered bottomrule. spacing — region-keyed blank-line control. named list keyed title / body / subgroup / footnote, named numeric c(= , = ) (footnote: ). Default Appendix-one blank line title block. Two adjoining region-sides target physical gap resolve MAX (never sum), gap never accidentally doubled. stripe — zebra body-row fills. single colour (applied even rows) named c(odd = , even = ); NULL (default) . indent_size — row-label indent width, monospace- space units. <integer(1)>. Default 2L. indent level adds many space-widths left padding cell. 0L disables indent prefix entirely. Backends native padding-left semantics (HTML / LaTeX / RTF / DOCX / PDF) emit cell padding wrapped continuation lines align indented baseline; Markdown carries literal space-prefix. Block alignment title / footnote / header / subgroup / body surfaces set via alignment named-list knob (alignment = list(title_halign = "left", ...)), scalar knob; blank-line spacing set via spacing (). na_text — global NA fallback. <character(1)>. decimal_metrics — decimal-padding metric. <character(1)>. "chars" (default); engine pads decimal columns character count. decimal_markers — missing-value tokens recognised col_spec(align = "decimal"). <character>. Default c("NR", "NE", "NC", "ND", "BLQ"). cell whose trimmed value one treated non-numeric marker: shown right-aligned column rather parsed number, marker appearing inside compound (e.g. upper bound 14.3 (11.2, NR)) preserved slot-aligned. Excludes "-", "NA", "INF"/"-INF" default: "-" collides range separators, "NA" handled na_text, infinities real values. Set character(0) disable marker handling. width_mode — table-level column-sizing policy. Mirrors Word's Table Layout menu. <character(1)>. One : "content" (default) — column auto-sized max(body, header). table fill page. Word's "Auto-fit Contents". "window" — Auto-sized columns expand share residual page width equally. Pinned percent columns keep pins. Word's "Auto-fit Window". "fixed" — explicit per-column widths drive layout. Auto-sized columns collapse minimum sliver. Word's "Fixed Column Width". Interaction: Pair col_spec(width = ...) pins drive layout "window" / "fixed". "content", pins still take priority auto columns. HTML backend. width_mode drives paper backends (LaTeX / RTF / PDF / DOCX) . HTML unconditionally responsive — table always fills parent columns wrap viewport narrows, regardless width_mode. Per-column widths (col_spec(width)) emit verbatim HTML colgroup per gt convention. whitespace — significant ASCII spaces labels cells render. <character(1)>. One : "preserve" (default) — leading, trailing, interior runs 2+ spaces become backend non-breaking token (  / ~ / \~; DOCX preserves via xml:space), hand-built indent like col_spec(label = "    Placebo") renders verbatim across every backend. single interior space stays breakable, cells still wrap. "collapse" — leave backend's native run-folding place (HTML / md / LaTeX collapse runs one space). Note: never affects col_spec(align = "decimal") padding, uses U+00A0 preserved unconditionally. footnote_markers — glyph scheme footnote() markers, engine allocates reading order. <character(1)>. One : "letters" (default) — , b, …, z, aa, ab, … (bijective base-26). "numbers" — 1, 2, 3, … "symbols" — Lamport's sequence *, †, ‡, §, ¶, ‖, doubled (**, ††, …) spills past sixth. Interaction: note's anchor fixed footnote(); scheme (knob) label (footnote_label) resolved active preset render, flipping either re-letters every marker . footnote_label — block-line template footnote() marker. <character(1)>. Default "{m}"; {m} token replaced allocated marker, "[{m}]" prints [] ahead note text footnote line. cell_padding — cell padding points, CSS shorthand length 1 / 2 / 4 (| vertical horizontal | top right bottom left), parsed length rule margins. <numeric>: default c(0, 5.4) (vertical 0, horizontal 5.4pt). single source truth auto column-width measurement (left + right) every backend's cell margin, measured rendered widths agree. Interaction: body per-side padding override (preset(padding = list(body = ...)) style(.= cells_body(), padding = ...)) takes precedence measurement render. Note: DOCX LaTeX render left / right exactly; RTF (\\trgaph one symmetric gap) renders average, total width still matches two sides look equal.   .template preset_spec bulk-apply .... <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. supplied, every knob template set away factory default feeds base layer; user-supplied ... knobs merge top. List-valued knobs (rules, fonts, colors, padding, alignment) shallow-merge per key; scalars replace. Use layer house-style preset_spec onto chain without restating knobs. .style style_template() layer onto cascade. <style_template | NULL>: default NULL. supplied, every layer template accumulated via style() replayed order engine time, per-spec style() layers .spec. Use attach sponsor's reusable house style chain without restating every per-region rule. .reset Discard spec's existing preset applying .... <logical(1)>: default FALSE. TRUE, spec's prior preset_spec () dropped ... knobs merged onto fresh preset_spec() defaults. knobs, per-spec preset cleared back NULL (spec falls set_preset() preset_spec() defaults).
code: preset(font_family = c("Courier New", "Courier", "Liberation Mono")) pagehead = list(   left   = "Protocol: ABC-123",   center = "Draft",   right  = "Page {page} of {npages}" ) pagehead = list(   left  = c("Protocol: ABC-123", "Analysis Set: Safety"),   right = "Page {page} of {npages}"   # scalar -> body-edge row ) # Landscape A4, 8pt body, slim margins for one wide table. preset(   orientation = "landscape",   paper_size  = "a4",   font_size   = 8,   margins     = c(0.75, 0.5, 0.75, 0.5) )

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining paginate(), style(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Override the render preset on a spec — preset
text: Per-spec, chained. preset() per-spec override — verb returns modified spec, composable pipe alongside cols() / headers() / paginate(). Use single table needs one-geometry (e.g. landscape A4 one wide efficacy summary inside portfolio portrait letter tables). Merge, replace. second preset() call merges scalar knobs onto spec's existing preset; unspecified knobs keep prior value. five named-list knobs (alignment / rules / fonts / colors / padding) lower style_layer records preset@style via .preset_args_to_layers() (internal) append call order; layer order precedence within engine cascade, later preset() call's lowered attribute wins earlier one cell. Pass .reset = TRUE discard existing knobs start preset_spec() defaults. preset(.spec, .reset = TRUE) knobs clears per-spec override entirely (spec falls set_preset() preset_spec() defaults render time). Direct preset_spec() calls bypass lowering. five named-list knobs longer slots preset_spec S7 class — exist preset() / set_preset() arguments lower @style. preset_spec(rules = list(...)) (analogous direct calls) raise "unused argument". Wrap calls tabular(...) |> preset(...) lowering helper fires layers land @style. Cascade set_preset(). engine resolves active preset order: (1) spec's per-call preset (verb), (2) session default attached via set_preset(), (3) preset_spec() factory defaults. first non-NULL layer wins; layers field-merged across cascade.

N={ne['placebo']}"),     drug_50    = col_spec(label = "Drug 50

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: Apply stripped-table look one verb. column-label divider (midrule) becomes rule drawn, every bold--default surface renders normal weight: title block, column-header band, subgroup banner, section-header rows synthesized usage = "group" columns. analogue ggplot2's theme_minimal(), composable pipe build verbs terminal emit() / as_grid().

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
code: preset_minimal(.spec, ...)

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: .spec tabular_spec apply minimal theme . <tabular_spec>: required. Dot-prefixed partial matching bind ... knob spec slot. ... Named preset knobs. Forwarded verbatim preset() (e.g. font_size, font_family, orientation, paper_size, margins), single call sets minimal look page geometry. Restriction: rules (legacy borders) knob owned helper may passed ; call preset() directly custom rule set.

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining paginate() / style(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Minimal theme: one header rule, normal weight throughout — preset_minimal
text: sets, theme (lowest) precedence explicit later style() wins: Rules. Drops booktabs toprule bottomrule (outer frame), keeping midrule column labels muted column-spanner spanrule. Equivalent preset(rules = list(toprule = "none", bottomrule = "none")). Weight. Sets bold = FALSE title, column-header, subgroup-label, group-header surfaces. HTML backend overrides font-weight: 600 class default inline font-weight: normal; paginated backends (RTF / LaTeX / PDF / DOCX) suppress surface's bold run. Last verb wins. weight layers ride theme tier, later explicit style(bold = TRUE, .= cells_title()) (surface) re-bolds . Treat preset_minimal() theme baseline override individual surfaces afterwards. Markdown. GFM represent colour / background / font surface; rendering styled surface .md emits one-time tabular_warning_fidelity degrades gracefully. Weight (bold) italic carry .

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Renders tabular_spec interactively. default behaviour mirrors gt::gt(): convert spec htmltools tag list let htmltools dispatch — RStudio + Positron viewer panes, Quarto / Rmd notebook inline, Databricks displayHTML, plain-console cat() handled without IDE- specific branching.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: x tabular_spec render. <tabular_spec>: required. object hand emit(). ... Forwarded htmltools::print / .tags(). Use pass id, style, class overrides wrapping <div>. view Open viewer? <logical(1)>: default interactive()“. role gt::gt's `view` argument: passes htmltools `browse = view`. Set `view = FALSE` suppress viewer one call (e.g. capture HTML string without launching window). output Force specific preview format. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL (auto). See output argument section full list. session default can set via options(tabular_print_output = "cli") users prefer structural summary HTML preview.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Invisibly returns x. Side effect: opens viewer, inlines chunk, cat()s output.

title: Print a tabular_spec — print.tabular_spec
text: Dispatch. print() delegates .tags.tabular_spec() returns htmltools::tagList. tag list handed htmltools's print method browse = view: htmltools opens IDE viewer one registered, inlines Quarto / Rmd chunk running inside one, cat()s HTML neither applies. is_rstudio() / is_positron() / is_notebook() heuristics — htmltools already knows. view argument. Defaults interactive(), universal -switch gt::gt() uses. Non-interactive contexts (Rscript, R CMD check, CI, devtools::test) bypass viewer automatically. Pass view = FALSE explicitly interactive prompt suppress viewer single call. output argument. Forces specific preview format instead default HTML-via-htmltools path. One : "html" — default, explicit. "md" / "markdown" — cat() markdown source console (round-trips backend_md). "latex" — cat() markdown source temporary placeholder (real LaTeX preview lands backend_latex). "rtf" / "docx" / "pdf" — render HTML preview emit cli note pointing emit() real artefact. viewer pane render RTF / OOXML, deliberately compile tinytex every autoprint. "cli" — print structural cli-tree summary (props, headers, sort, pagination, preset). Useful debugging spec composition without paying HTML render cost. Robustness. HTML render wrapped tryCatch; rendering fails reason printer falls back cli-tree summary cli::cli_warn() describing failure, broken spec never crashes REPL. Tempdir. Preview HTML files live getOption("tabular_preview_dir", default = tempdir()). Override option keep stable location (handy Linux distros browsers read access /tmp/).

what: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: Stash preset_spec package-internal session environment. Every subsequent tabular() chain attach preset() inherits knobs render time. Mirrors ggplot2's ggplot2::theme_set(): one call front, many tables downstream.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
code: set_preset(new = NULL, ..., .template = NULL, .style = NULL, .reset = FALSE)

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: new preset_spec install wholesale. <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. non-NULL, replaces session preset one call without touching knobs. primary use save/restore round-trip (old <- set_preset(...); set_preset(old)) — new accepts preset_spec previously returned set_preset() get_preset(). Mutually exclusive ..., .template, .style, .reset: passing alongside non-NULL new raises tabular_error_input. ... Named preset knobs. shape preset(); see verb full list 13 recognised knobs. Unknown names raise tabular_error_input. Mutually exclusive non-NULL new. .template preset_spec bulk-apply .... <preset_spec | NULL>: default NULL. semantics preset()'s .template: every knob set away factory default feeds base layer; user-supplied ... knobs merge top shallow-merge per list-valued knob. .style style_template() layer session default. <style_template | NULL>: default NULL. semantics preset()'s .style: template's accumulated layers feed session-default style, layered per-spec style() calls. .reset Discard existing session preset applying .... <logical(1)>: default FALSE. knobs, clears session default back NULL.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: previous session preset_spec (invisible). Returns NULL session preset attached prior call. Capture round-trip temporary override: old <- set_preset(...); set_preset(old). Mirrors ggplot2::theme_set() base::options() — canonical tidyverse save/restore primitive.

title: Set or clear the session default preset — set_preset
text: Persistence. session preset lives package-internal environment populated tabular loaded emptied namespace unloads. -disk persistence; set default top analysis script (project-level .Rprofile) sticky house style needed. Merge, replace. second set_preset() call merges knobs onto existing session preset; unspecified knobs keep prior value. Pass .reset = TRUE discard existing session preset start preset_spec() defaults. set_preset(.reset = TRUE) knobs clears session default back NULL. Save restore. Every call returns previous session preset invisibly, primitive ggplot2's ggplot2::theme_set() ships. Capture , render, restore passing saved value back positional new argument:   prior NULL (session preset ever attached), restore set_preset(.reset = TRUE) instead — set_preset(NULL) shape set_preset() falls factory defaults rather clearing session. Cascade preset(). per-spec preset() always wins session default. session default fills spec carries preset .

the # second half produces efficacy tables at 10pt on landscape A4. A # single `set_preset(.reset = TRUE, ...)` resets the cascade before # the second batch starts. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") get_preset()@font_size  # 9 #> [1] 9  set_preset(   .reset      = TRUE,   font_size   = 10,   orientation = "landscape",   paper_size  = "a4" ) get_preset()@orientation  # "landscape" #> [1] "landscape"  # Reset the session default so subsequent examples / R sessions # are not affected. set_preset(.reset = TRUE)  # ---- Example 3: Save and restore around a renegade table ---- # # Most of the submission renders portrait letter at 9pt. One # renegade efficacy table needs landscape A4 at 10pt. Capture # the prior session preset, render the renegade, then restore. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter")  old <- set_preset(   font_size   = 10,   paper_size  = "a4",   orientation = "landscape" ) # ... one renegade render ... if (is.null(old)) {   set_preset(.reset = TRUE)   # was no prior — clear } else {   set_preset(old)              # round-trip via the positional `new` arg } get_preset()@paper_size  # "letter" — restored #> [1] "letter"  # ---- Example 4: Snapshot current preset, mutate, restore ---- # # Capture whatever the session preset is right now (may be NULL), # let a downstream helper mutate it, then put it back when done. set_preset(font_size = 9, paper_size = "letter") snapshot <- get_preset()  # Simulate downstream code mutating session state. set_preset(font_size = 11, orientation = "landscape")  # Restore. The wholesale-install path of `set_preset(new)` # accepts any `preset_spec` returned by `get_preset()` / # `set_preset()`. if (is.null(snapshot)) {   set_preset(.reset = TRUE) } else {   set_preset(snapshot) } get_preset()@font_size    # 9 — restored #> [1] 9  # Reset for subsequent examples / R sessions. set_preset(.reset = TRUE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Attach sort_spec tabular_spec. engine applies sort pagination, may reference column spec@data whether column declared cols().

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
code: sort_rows(.spec, by = character(), descending = FALSE)

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: .spec tabular_spec attach sort . <tabular_spec>: required. Ordered column names sort , precedence order. <character>: default character(). Length 0 accepted (-op sort). May reference columns declared cols() — sort-helper columns ride along engine. Restriction: Every entry must column spec@data. reference arm columns produced pivot_across(); pivot upstream sort instead. Arm cells hold rendered stat strings (e.g. "75.2 (8.3)") order meaningfully.   descending Per-key sort direction. <logical(1) | logical(length())>: default FALSE. TRUE sorts corresponding key descending; length 1 recycles every key. Restriction: NAs. Length must 1 length(). Tip: mixed-direction multi-key sorts, pass length() values; engine inverts xtfrm rank descending key calls order() keys.
code: # Two-key clinical sort: row_type ascending, n_total descending. sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE))

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining style(), paginate(), preset(), render via emit() (resolve without /O via as_grid()).

title: Sort the display rows — sort_rows
text: Replace, stack. second sort_rows() call REPLACES prior sort — sort single spec, stackable list. Call arguments clear. NA last, regardless direction. NA values sort key placed end whether key ascending descending (matching order(..., na.last = TRUE)). Factor levels drive order. Factor columns sort factor levels, character label. CDISC BOR ordering (CR < PR < SD < NON-CR/NON-PD < PD < NE < MISSING) survives tabular pipeline without explicit mutate() — coerce stat_label factor levels clinical order upstream, sort_rows(= "stat_label") rest.

what: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: One verb, one cascade. style() call appends single style_layer (location + style attributes) spec template. Layers accumulate declaration order; engine merges render time later layers win per attribute, NA-valued fields leave prior layer intact.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
code: style(.spec, ..., .at = cells_body())

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: .spec tabular_spec tabular_style_template. <tabular_spec | tabular_style_template>: required. Dot-prefixed R's partial argument matching accidentally bind short attribute name ... spec slot. piping style_template() |> style(...) layers accumulate onto template instead spec. ... Named style attributes. least one required. See vocabulary list recognised names. .Location object selecting surface layer targets. <tabular_location>: default cells_body(). Build one cells_*() constructors; see cells_body() siblings. Dot-prefixed (tidyverse convention) comes ... — way user-passed style attribute can never collide arg's name.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: updated tabular_spec (tabular_style_template, called one).

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: Locations. argument selects surface layer targets. Every region rendered page cells_*() constructor: cells_body()             — body cells (default) cells_headers()          — column header band cells_group_headers()    — synthetic group-header rows cells_title()            — title block cells_subgroup_labels()  — subgroup banner row cells_footnotes()        — footnote block cells_pagehead()         — page-header band cells_pagefoot()         — page-footer band cells_table()            — table-wide regions (outer borders, body-row separators) Body filters live cells_body(): = 1:3 integer-index rows, j = "Total" column-name targeting, = <expr> quosure-captured predicate evaluated spec@data. Attribute vocabulary. layer carries style_node built .... Recognised attribute names: Text — bold, italic, underline, color, background, font_family, font_size Alignment — halign ("left" / "center" / "right"), valign ("top" / "middle" / "bottom") Borders — border (umbrella), border_top, border_bottom, border_left, border_right (takes brdr() value literal "none"); per-side scalars border_<side>_{style,width,color} finer control Padding — padding (scalar applies four sides; named vector c(top = , right = , bottom = , left = ) sets side); per-side scalars padding_<side> directly Spacing — blank_above, blank_below (integer blank lines / block — cells_title() / cells_footnotes() / cells_subgroup_labels()) Inline — pretext, posttext (literal text prepended / appended around cell value) Unknown attribute names emit cli::cli_warn drop constructed node; engine never sees foreign property.

title: Attach a style layer to a tabular_spec or style_template — style
text: Adverse Events SOC / PT
code: # ---- AE table by SOC and PT with per-row indent + styled hierarchy ---- # `cdisc_saf_aesocpt` ships with `indent_level` (0 on overall/SOC rows, # 1 on PT rows); `col_spec(indent_by = "indent_level")` drives the # PT indent on the `label` column. tabular(cdisc_saf_aesocpt, titles = "Adverse Events by SOC / PT",         footnotes = "") |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "Category", align = "left",                         indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   # SOC summary rows bolded (depth 0 — flush)   style(bold = TRUE,         .at = cells_body(where = row_type == "soc")) |>   # Overall row gets a light background   style(background = "#f0f0f0",         .at = cells_body(where = row_type == "overall"))  #tabular-3b7b8aca0f { what: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: Build reusable, composable style template chaining style() calls tabular_style_template. template carries ordered list style_layer records can attached preset() / set_preset() style = argument — every downstream tabular() chain inherits template's layers via engine cascade.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: style_template()  is_style_template(x)

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: x R object. predicate inspects class via inherits(); introspection performed.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: tabular_style_template — small S3 list layers slot. Pipe style() add layers.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
text: One verb, two surfaces. style(.spec_or_template, ..., .= ...) call attaches layer per-table spec also accumulates layers onto template. Symmetric API — need learn second function multi-table use case. Submission workflow. submission typically renders 100–200 tables one visual identity. Build template top submission script, pass set_preset(style = template), every subsequent tabular() produces output inherits column-header rules, group-header bolding, title spacing, outer-frame borders without single per-table style() call. Cascade order. Engines apply layers low--high priority: backend defaults → session preset's @style → spec preset's @style → per-spec style() layers. Later layers override prior ones per attribute; NA fields leave prior layer's value place.

title: Reusable style template (for house-style presets) — style_template
code: # ---- Sponsor "house style" composed once ---- # # The result becomes the default look for every table rendered # against this preset. No per-table style() boilerplate. house <- style_template() |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_headers(level = -1)) |>   style(bold = TRUE, .at = cells_group_headers()) |>   style(     border_top    = brdr("thick", "double"),     border_bottom = brdr("thick", "double"),     .at = cells_headers()   ) |>   style(blank_above = 1, blank_below = 1, .at = cells_title())  length(house$layers) #> [1] 4  # ---- Verify class ---- is_style_template(house) #> [1] TRUE

what: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Attach subgroup_spec tabular_spec. render time engine partitions spec@data unique values , runs full resolve pipeline per group, concatenates results. hard page break inserted groups — every subgroup value starts page. centred banner line appears column-header rule every page group (including continuation pages), matching canonical submission page-layout convention.

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
code: subgroup(.spec, by, label = NULL, big_n = NULL, big_n_fmt = "\n(N={n})")

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: .spec tabular_spec partition. <tabular_spec>: required. Column name(s) partition . <character>: required. Must reference column spec@data. Length-0 (character(0)) clears partition. Matches = arg convention sort_rows(). Multi-variable. Pass c("var1", "var2") cross every combination present data. Multi-var partitions require explicit label template (single-var auto-default generalise). label Banner template. <character(1) | NULL>: default NULL. Glue-style template {column_name} placeholders. NULL derives default partition variable's attr(data[[]], "label") (falling back column name). Tip: reference auxiliary columns inline BigN qualifier constant within group — e.g. "Cohort: {cohort} (N = {n})". Restriction: Every {col} reference must column spec@data. Unknown columns raise tabular_error_subgroup_template_unknown_col. big_n Per-page BigN denominators. <data.frame> | NULL: default NULL. table giving (N=x) denominator arm's header show subgroup page. arm named appears header — either data column (N rides column's leaf label) headers() band label (N rides spanner band). Ns non-negative whole numbers; provide one per combination present data. Accepts either shape: Wide — column(s) plus one numeric column per arm (cells Ns). Long — column(s) plus one arm-name column one numeric N column, .e. dplyr::count() / summarise() output used directly reshaping.   Requirement: band keying needs headers() subgroup() pipeline; arm name must resolve exactly one leaf XOR one band. Every missing per-page N call-time error, never silently wrong denominator. Note: per-arm N renders every backend. paged backends (RTF, PDF / LaTeX, DOCX) carry column header repeats every page subgroup. HTML Markdown continuous (one stacked table, one header), instead emit per-arm N row directly subgroup banner, (N=x) aligned beneath arm column. big_n_fmt Per-page BigN template. <character(1)>: default "\n(N={n})". Appended arm's header label, {n} substituted page/column's integer N. {n} token allowed; default puts N line arm name.
code: # Wide: one column per arm. wide <- data.frame(   sex     = factor(c("F", "M")),   placebo = c(24L, 18L), drug_50 = c(9L, 15L), Total = c(42L, 47L) ) # Long: count()-style, pivoted internally. Equivalent to `wide`. long <- data.frame(   sex  = factor(rep(c("F", "M"), each = 3)),   arm  = rep(c("placebo", "drug_50", "Total"), 2),   n    = c(24L, 9L, 42L, 18L, 15L, 47L) ) spec |> subgroup(by = "sex", big_n = long)

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: updated tabular_spec. Continue chaining resolve via as_grid() / emit().

title: Partition the report by a variable — subgroup
text: Label glue-style template. label carries {col} placeholders, engine substitutes placeholder FIRST ROW group's filtered data — column whose value constant within group (BigN, cohort descriptor, qualifier text) can ride banner. Columns vary within group also resolve, always first row's value; pre-compute aggregates upstream. Default label (label = NULL, single var): engine generates "<attr(data[[]], 'label') %||% >: {<>}", subgroup(= "cohort") renders banners like "Cohort: " "Cohort: B" without configuration. Replace, stack. second subgroup() call REPLACES prior partition — subgroup single spec, stackable list. Passing = character(0) clears slot, though typical clinical pipelines set partition front. Display-side . subgroup() partitions pre-summarised wide data frame; aggregate, filter, weight. user supplies one summary row per displayed row per group; tabular's job solely lay per-group banner page break. Multi-variable crossing. = c("SEX", "AGEGR1") partitions every combination present data (first variable varies slowest, matching expand.grid() convention). explicit label template required multi-var partitions since single-var default "<var>: {<var>}" generalise; raise tabular_error_subgroup_label_required otherwise. Auto-hide partition + template columns. Every column named , plus every column referenced via {col} placeholder label, automatically flips visible = FALSE engine time. Users restate col_spec(visible = FALSE) inside cols() columns — mirroring col_spec(indent_by = ...) auto-hide ergonomic.

code: # ---- Example 1: TEAEs by treatment arm — one set of pages per arm ---- # # Partition the AE-by-SOC/PT pipeline by treatment arm. Each arm # value gets its own page set with a centred `Treatment Arm: <value>` # banner above the column-header rule on every page, separated by # hard page breaks. The default label uses the variable's `label` # attribute when present, falling back to the column name. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) ae <- cdisc_saf_aesocpt ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) ae$n_total  <- as.integer(sub(" .*", "", ae$Total)) attr(ae$row_type, "label") <- "Row Type"  tabular(   ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by SOC and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = "Subjects counted once per SOC and once per PT." ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo",  align = "decimal"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50",  align = "decimal"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100", align = "decimal"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total",    align = "decimal")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE)) |>   subgroup(by = "row_type")  #tabular-3431c78506 { what: tabular: Render Tables and Listings for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
title: tabular: Render Tables and Listings for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
text: Render clinical submission tables listings 'RTF', 'LaTeX', 'HTML', 'PDF', 'DOCX' pre-summarised data frames, external 'Java' 'SAS' dependency. Features include decimal alignment via font metrics, multi-level column headers passthrough leaves, predicate-targeted cell styling, footnotes, group-aware pagination. Built Clinical Data Interchange Standards Consortium (CDISC) Analysis Data Model (ADaM) workflows regulatory submissions agencies Food Drug Administration (FDA), European Medicines Agency (EMA), Pharmaceuticals Medical Devices Agency (PMDA).

title: tabular: Render Tables and Listings for Clinical Submissions — tabular-package
text: Maintainer: Vignesh Thanikachalam .vignesh@gmail.com

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Wrap pre-summarised data frame tabular_spec ready verb chain. tabular() entry verb — owns data, titles, footnotes slots; every downstream verb (cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset()) returns updated spec chaining, terminating emit() (write file) as_grid() (resolve without writing).

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
code: tabular(data, titles = NULL, footnotes = NULL)

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: data display rows. <data.frame>: required. Pre-summarised wide-format data; tibbles, data.tables, arrow tables coerced via .data.frame(). Factor columns preserved (levels drive sort_rows()). Restriction: least one column; column names must unique. Zero rows accepted (engine renders "data" stub). Interaction: cards-format counterparts (cdisc_saf_demo_ard, cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard) accepted directly; pipe pivot_across() first. titles Page-title block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. element renders centred line; embedded \n wraps within row. backend collapses unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title. Restriction: NAs. element supports glue-style {expr} interpolation: braces evaluated R code calling environment build time, e.g. "N total = {sum(n)}". Double brace ({{ }}) literal one. md() / html() element passed without interpolation.   footnotes Page-footnote block, one element per row. <character> | NULL: default NULL. User-supplied prose rows ; backend appends program-path / program-name / timestamp band render time. Restriction: NAs. element supports glue-style {expr} interpolation (see titles).
code: # Canonical 3-line title block with BigN-qualified population. n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short) titles = c(   "Table 14.3.1",   "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",   "Safety Population" ) # Canonical 3-line footnote block. footnotes = c(   "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.",   "Percentages based on N per treatment group.",   "TEAE = treatment-emergent adverse event." )

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: tabular_spec S7 object. Pipe cols(), headers(), sort_rows(), style(), paginate(), preset() build display, emit() render as_grid() resolve without writing.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Pre-summarised input contract. data one row per displayed row final table. tabular() aggregate, filter, weight, generate subtotal rows — happen upstream cards, dplyr, SAS. upstream long cards::ard_stack() ARD, pipe pivot_across() first land wide shape tabular() accepts. Multi-line titles footnotes contract. Clinical tables routinely carry 2-4 title rows 1-4 user footnote rows. Pass row one element character vector; backend renders element line, collapsing unused rows column-header band sits flush lowest used title.

title: Start a tabular display — tabular
text: Table 14.3.1 Adverse Events System Organ Class Preferred Term Safety Population   Subjects counted per SOC per PT. Percentages based N per treatment group.   Table 14.2.1 Best Overall Response Response Rates Efficacy Evaluable Population   Response per RECIST 1.1, investigator assessment.   Safety-population BigN per arm     Table 14.4.1 Summary Vital Signs Safety Population   Statistics computed observed cases.
code:
# ---- Example 1: Adverse-event table by SOC and Preferred Term ---- # # The regulatory work-horse layout: AE-by-SOC/PT with the # canonical 3-line title block (table number, description, # population qualifier with BigN drawn inline from `cdisc_saf_n`) and a # two-line footnote block explaining the denominator. The # downstream pipeline hides the hierarchy markers (`row_type`, # `soc_n`, `n_total`) but keeps them in the data so `sort_rows()` # can arrange SOCs and PTs in descending order of subject count. # The dataset already ships `n_total` and `soc_n`; here we slice to # the overall row plus the two highest-incidence SOCs to keep the # preview compact. ae <- cdisc_saf_aesocpt keep_soc <- head(unique(ae$soc[ae$row_type == "soc"]), 2L) ae <- ae[ae$row_type == "overall" | ae$soc %in% keep_soc, ] ae$row_type <- factor(ae$row_type, levels = c("overall", "soc", "pt")) n <- stats::setNames(cdisc_saf_n$n, cdisc_saf_n$arm_short)  tabular(   ae,   titles = c(     "Table 14.3.1",     "Adverse Events by System Organ Class and Preferred Term",     "Safety Population"   ),   footnotes = c(     "Subjects are counted once per SOC and once per PT.",     "Percentages based on N per treatment group."   ) ) |>   cols(     label    = col_spec(label = "SOC / PT", indent_by = "indent_level"),     soc      = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     soc_n    = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     row_type = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     n_total  = col_spec(visible = FALSE),     placebo  = col_spec(label = "Placebo
N={n['placebo']}"),     drug_50  = col_spec(label = "Drug 50
N={n['drug_50']}"),     drug_100 = col_spec(label = "Drug 100
N={n['drug_100']}"),     Total    = col_spec(label = "Total
N={n['Total']}")   ) |>   sort_rows(by = c("row_type", "n_total"), descending = c(FALSE, TRUE))  #tabular-c5d3696069 { font-family: "Liberation Mono", "Courier New", Courier, monospace; color: #212529; title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: S7 class definitions backing tabular's display-side IR. Users construct directly except col_spec(); every class built chained verb pipeline (tabular() -> cols() -> headers() -> sort_rows() -> style() -> paginate() -> preset() -> as_grid() / emit()).

title: tabular S7 classes — tabular_classes
text: class set intentionally small (~11 concepts) IR fits one mental model: Every spec slot typed: verb mutate slot invalid value fails construction time (S7 validator runs last-line defense behind cli-friendly verb-level validators). Class predicates. class matching is_<name>() predicate; see tabular_predicates full list.

what: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
title: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
text: Class predicates returning single logical indicating whether x inherits corresponding tabular S7 class. Use gate user-side code branches verb returned, write defensive helpers wrap tabular pipelines, assert intermediate shapes pipeline debugging.

text: x Object test. R value. predicate returns TRUE x inherits named class, FALSE otherwise.

title: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
text: single TRUE / FALSE. Use / stopifnot guards, chain validation helpers. length-1 logical — TRUE FALSE. Never NA.

title: Test for tabular S7 class instances — tabular_predicates
text: Eleven predicates cover full S7 surface: Predicates never error — return FALSE NULL, vectors, objects class, S7 objects packages. Use layer user's pipeline without defensive tryCatch().

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: First release. tabular renders pre-summarised clinical tables listings RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF, DOCX one immutable verb pipeline, external Java SAS dependency. initial CRAN version consolidates entire pre-release development cycle: every feature bug fix designed, implemented, tested first release. Scope. release covers tables listings. Figure (graph) output yet supported focus next release.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: tabular() takes pre-summarised wide data frame (one input row one display row) plus multi-line titles footnotes. cols() / col_spec() set per-column usage, label, format, width, alignment (including decimal alignment via bundled font metrics), visibility, NA text, per-row indent (indent_by), group display. cols() takes .default fallback col_spec(), cols_apply() applies one col_spec() many columns (name predicate), resolving {.name} token label matched column. headers() builds multi-level column-header bands passthrough leaves; sort_rows() sorts hidden numeric keys; subgroup() partitions {col} banner template optional per-page big_n. style() applies predicate-targeted cell styling cells_*() location helpers; preset() / set_preset() / get_preset() set cosmetic defaults (fonts, colours, rules, padding, alignment, page chrome) per table per session, reusable house styles via style_template(). footnote() attaches auto-numbered footnotes (letters, numbers, symbols) cell, column header, title, deduped id byte-identical across backends. paginate() group-aware pagination auto-computed per-page row budget; emit() writes chosen backend (creating parent directories create_dir), as_grid() resolves grid without /O.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: Native emission RTF, LaTeX, HTML, PDF (via LaTeX), DOCX single resolved grid; output verified per-backend byte snapshots cross-backend CDISC-pilot qualification (inst/qualification/). Inline markup via md() html(). Glue-style {expr} interpolation static labels, titles, footnotes, header band labels, evaluated calling environment build time. Significant-whitespace preservation across backends (preset(whitespace = "preserve"), default). check_latex() reports LaTeX-package availability PDF output prints exact tlmgr_install() remedy anything missing — including CTAN-mirror pin (tinytex::tlmgr_repo("auto")) install stalls behind redirecting default mirror (commonly Windows); check_fonts() fonts, per backend.

title: tabular 0.1.0
text: Bundled synthetic CDISC-pilot demo data (cdisc_saf_demo, cdisc_saf_ae, cdisc_saf_aesocpt, cdisc_saf_vital, cdisc_eff_resp, BigN frames cdisc_saf_n / cdisc_eff_n, long-format ARD companions cdisc_saf_demo_ard / cdisc_saf_aesocpt_ard) examples vignettes.
